retry_reason: The function destroys references; if a field is NULL it is a no-op. If a field is non-NULL, the put decrements the refcount. Retrying would cause double-put (use-after-free) on the already released objects, so retry is never appropriate.
contract_summary: Releases all reference-counted objects held by a drm_connector_state. This is the cleanup/destroy phase: it decrements refcounts on the connector, the crtc_commit, and the hdr_output_metadata blob, and cleans up the writeback_job if present. After calling this function, the state must not be used for any purposes. The caller must have previously acquired references (e.g., via drm_connector_get, drm_crtc_commit_get, drm_property_blob_get) for each field that is non-NULL. This function is typically called immediately before freeing the state memory or as part of state replacement in atomic commit teardown.
